chain_name: Tier 2 Triage — Feb Report Remaining Open Items
methodology: Research-first: explicit code flow mapping via code review. Trace events/functions end-to-end. Verdict: VALID BUG, BY DESIGN, SUPERSEDED, or NON-ISSUE. Implementation planned only after research confirms need.

sessions.title: CW-2: Orchestrator Cannot Reactivate (reopen_job)
sessions.investigation_question: Does 0498 handover protocol + 0497e successor spawning already cover orchestrator reactivation? Or is a reopen_job() MCP tool genuinely needed?
sessions.verdict_reasoning: The original CW-2 concern (no reopen_job() tool) is fully superseded by two mechanisms: (1) 0497e successor spawning with predecessor_job_id injects completion context into a NEW fresh agent, and (2) 0498 handover/continuation protocol allows same-identity session refresh for orchestrators. A reopen_job() tool would conflict with the architectural principle of Job/Execution separation and would require violating a database CHECK constraint. The system is designed so that completed work is immutable and new work gets a new Job+Execution pair.
code_flows_traced: SPAWN: spawn_agent_job() [orchestration_service.py:361-566] -> creates AgentJob(status=active) + AgentExecution(status=waiting) -> WebSocket broadcast
START: get_agent_mission() [orchestration_service.py:952-1194] -> filters NOT_IN(complete,decommissioned) -> transitions waiting->working atomically on first fetch
PROGRESS: report_progress() [orchestration_service.py:1278-1506] -> filters NOT_IN(complete,decommissioned) -> updates progress/todos but DOES NOT change execution.status
ERROR: report_error() [orchestration_service.py:1893-1996] -> sets execution.status=blocked + block_reason -> WebSocket broadcast
COMPLETE: complete_job() [orchestration_service.py:1559-1807] -> sets execution.status=complete + job.status=completed (if last execution) -> auto-sends completion_report to orchestrator

SUCCESSOR: spawn_agent_job(predecessor_job_id=X) [orchestration_service.py:361-566] -> _build_predecessor_context() [568-674] -> injects predecessor summary+commits into new mission -> NEW AgentJob + NEW AgentExecution
HANDOVER: simple_handover endpoint [agent_jobs/simple_handover.py:41-186] -> build_retirement_prompt() + build_continuation_prompt() -> SAME agent_id/job_id, new terminal
SEND_MESSAGE: MessageService.send_message() [message_service.py:122-357] -> resolves recipients via status.in_(waiting,working,blocked) -> CANNOT deliver to complete or decommissioned agents

state_machine.AgentJob_statuses: active -> completed (via complete_job) | active -> cancelled (reserved). CHECK constraint: IN(active,completed,cancelled). ONE-WAY transitions only.
state_machine.AgentExecution_statuses: waiting -> working -> complete | working -> blocked -> complete | any -> decommissioned. CHECK constraint: IN(waiting,working,blocked,complete,silent,decommissioned). No reverse from complete/decommissioned.

findings.scenario_1_more_work_for_completed_agent: Covered by 0497e successor spawning. New AgentJob+AgentExecution created with predecessor context. Architecturally superior: fresh context prevents hallucination drift, completed result is immutable audit trail.
findings.scenario_2_unblock_blocked_agent: Partially covered. Blocked agents CAN still call get_agent_mission(), report_progress(), complete_job(). Orchestrator CAN send messages to blocked agents. Minor cosmetic gap: no mechanism to set dashboard status from blocked back to working.
findings.scenario_3_continuation_orchestrator: Fully covered by 0498 handover. Same agent_id/job_id, new terminal, reads 360 Memory for context.

sessions.notes_for_next: CW-2 is SUPERSEDED. No implementation needed. Minor cosmetic gap (blocked->working status transition) does not warrant a new tool. If desired in future: ~2 hours including tests.

sessions.title: CW-4: Duration Timer Not Reactivated
sessions.investigation_question: What timer mechanism exists today? Where is duration tracked? What happens during handover? Is there a real user-facing problem?
sessions.verdict_reasoning: Duration is tracked PER-EXECUTION via AgentExecution.started_at/completed_at. Simple handover (0498) reuses the SAME execution row (same UUID, same card). Timer keeps running across sessions. No pause/resume needed because nothing is paused. Project.paused_at field exists but is NEVER written to. The paused status was removed from Project enum in Handover 0071. Frontend formatDuration() correctly computes elapsed time from started_at for working agents. No project-level duration display exists.
code_flows_traced: AgentExecution model: src/giljo_mcp/models/agent_identity.py lines 180-181 (started_at, completed_at)
AgentJob model: src/giljo_mcp/models/agent_identity.py lines 82-83 (created_at, completed_at)
Project model: src/giljo_mcp/models/projects.py lines 123-135 (created_at, activated_at, paused_at, completed_at)
Timer start: orchestration_service.py line 1084 (execution.started_at = now on first mission fetch)
Timer stop: orchestration_service.py line 1704 (execution.completed_at on complete_job)
API response: api/endpoints/agent_jobs/status.py lines 68-69 (started_at/completed_at in JobResponse)
Service serialization: orchestration_service.py lines 2148-2149 (ISO format from AgentExecution)
Frontend formatter: frontend/src/components/projects/JobsTab.vue lines 606-636 (formatDuration)
Frontend timer: JobsTab.vue lines 525-562 (setInterval 1s updating now ref)
Handover: api/endpoints/agent_jobs/simple_handover.py line 66 (Does NOT create new AgentExecution rows)

timer_architecture.computation: Frontend-computed: completed_at - started_at (finished) or Date.now() - started_at (working)
timer_architecture.display: JobsTab.vue Duration column only. No project-level duration display.
timer_architecture.no_duration_field: No duration field on any model. Always computed from timestamp pairs.

handover_behavior.simple_handover: Same AgentExecution row reused. started_at unchanged. completed_at stays null.
handover_behavior.no_new_execution: 0498 handover does NOT create new AgentExecution. Same agent_id, job_id, card.
handover_behavior.no_pause_state: Project.paused_at exists but NEVER written. paused removed from status enum (0071).

user_impact.severity: None. Timer works correctly across handovers.
user_impact.what_user_sees: Working agent shows live counter from original started_at. Completed shows final duration.
user_impact.edge_case: Browser close/reopen recalculates correctly from DB-persisted started_at.

sessions.notes_for_next: Original CW-4 may have been written before 0498 simple handover unified the approach. If project-level duration tracking is desired in the future, Project.activated_at and Project.completed_at already exist for that purpose.

sessions.title: #40+#41: Workflow Status Orchestrator Distinction + Per-Agent Status
sessions.investigation_question: What does get_workflow_status() currently return? Does it already distinguish orchestrator from sub-agents? Does it already include per-agent status?
sessions.verdict: #40: VALID (minor, P3/E1) | #41: SUPERSEDED by commit a00a863e
sessions.verdict_reasoning: #40: AgentJob.job_type stores 'orchestrator' in DB and the JOIN fetches it, but AgentWorkflowDetail schema discards it. Fix is ~3 lines across 2 files. Practical need is LOW — orchestrator already knows its team composition. #41: Commit a00a863e (Feb 28 2026) added agents list with per-agent job_id, agent_id, agent_name, display_name, status, unread_messages, and todos breakdown. Fully resolved via MCP tool response.
code_flows_traced: MCP tool def: api/endpoints/mcp_http.py:640-654
Tool dispatch: api/endpoints/mcp_http.py:875 -> tool_accessor.get_workflow_status
Tool accessor: src/giljo_mcp/tools/tool_accessor.py:467-473 (pass-through)
Service: src/giljo_mcp/services/orchestration_service.py:189-355
DB query: orchestration_service.py:237-250 (JOIN AgentExecution+AgentJob)
Per-agent detail: orchestration_service.py:293-330 (batch todo query)
Schema: src/giljo_mcp/schemas/service_responses.py:292-332
MCP serialization: mcp_http.py:985 (model_dump -> json.dumps)
REST endpoint: api/endpoints/agent_jobs/orchestration.py:80-120 (drops agents array)
Orchestrator job_type: project_service.py:1228,2090 (job_type=orchestrator)
Sub-agent job_type: orchestration_service.py:468 (job_type=agent_display_name)

#40_orchestrator_distinction.current_state: NOT in response. job_type stored in DB and fetched via JOIN but not surfaced in AgentWorkflowDetail.
#40_orchestrator_distinction.workaround: Orchestrator knows its own job_ids. agent_name often contains 'orchestrator'. exclude_job_id removes self.
#40_orchestrator_distinction.fix_scope: E1: Add job_type field to AgentWorkflowDetail + assign in service loop (~3 lines, 2 files)

#41_per_agent_status.current_state: RESOLVED by commit a00a863e. agents array includes job_id, agent_id, agent_name, display_name, status, unread_messages, todos.
#41_per_agent_status.note: REST API WorkflowStatusResponse does NOT include agents array (separate frontend concern)

response_schema.top_level_fields: active_agents, completed_agents, pending_agents, blocked_agents, silent_agents, decommissioned_agents, current_stage, progress_percent, total_agents, caller_note, agents[]
response_schema.per_agent_fields: job_id, agent_id, agent_name, display_name, status, unread_messages, todos{completed, in_progress, pending, skipped}

sessions.implementation_needed: #40: Optional P3/E1 — add job_type to AgentWorkflowDetail. Not blocking. #41: None.
sessions.notes_for_next: If #40 pursued: add job_type:str to AgentWorkflowDetail, extract from rows in service loop. Also consider adding phase:int for multi-terminal context. REST API agents-array gap is separate enhancement.

sessions.title: #50: Platform Detection cmd.exe vs GNU bash
sessions.investigation_question: Where does platform detection happen? Where is timeout vs sleep used? Check if 0497a thin prompt or 0497c orchestrator prompt already fixed this.
sessions.verdict_reasoning: protocol_builder.py embeds incorrect platform-specific shell commands in both orchestrator and sub-agent prompts. It tells agents to use Windows cmd.exe 'timeout /t N /nobreak' when they detect Windows, but Claude Code on Windows runs in Git Bash where 'sleep' works and 'timeout' does NOT. Practical impact is minimal: LLMs detect their shell, 0804b removed prescriptive polling, sub-agents never need sleep. The entire STEP 0 block is ~60 wasted tokens per prompt.
code_flows_traced: protocol_builder._build_ch2_startup() lines 576-582: Orchestrator STEP 0 has incorrect 'timeout /t N /nobreak' for Windows
protocol_builder._generate_agent_protocol() lines 236-240: Sub-agent Phase 1 has identical incorrect timeout command
thin_prompt_generator.py line 148: Says 'sleep' generically (not platform-specific, OK)
AgentTipsDialog.vue lines 54,59,72: Says 'bash sleep' explicitly (correct, targets bash)
AgentTipsDialog.vue lines 125,131,140: powershell.exe spawn commands (Windows-specific by design)
slash_command_templates.py lines 74-76: Correctly says Unix paths work on ALL platforms

problematic_locations.issue: Orchestrator CH2 STEP 0 includes incorrect 'timeout /t N /nobreak' for Windows
Sub-agent Phase 1 ENVIRONMENT DETECTION has identical incorrect platform reference

non_problematic: thin_prompt_generator.py line 148: generic sleep reference, OK
slash_command_templates.py lines 74-76: correctly says Unix paths work everywhere

prior_fixes.0497a: Replaced verbose agent prompt with thin prompt. Did NOT remove platform detection from protocol.
prior_fixes.0804b: Removed prescriptive polling loops. Did NOT touch platform detection block.

findings.backend_platform_detection: Extensive in install.py, startup.py, control_panel.py, installer/platforms/ for server-side ops. Correct and unrelated.
findings.git_bash_sleep_works: Confirmed: sleep N works in Git Bash on Windows. timeout /t N /nobreak does NOT.

implementation_needed.scope: Remove STEP 0/ENVIRONMENT DETECTION blocks from _build_ch2_startup() and _generate_agent_protocol(). Replace with single line: Your shell supports standard Unix commands regardless of OS.

sessions.notes_for_next: Delete platform detection blocks from _build_ch2_startup() (lines 576-582) and _generate_agent_protocol() (lines 236-240). Replace with one-liner about Unix commands. Saves ~60 tokens per prompt.

chain_summary: All 4 Tier 2 research sessions complete. Results: CW-2 SUPERSEDED (0497e+0498 cover it), CW-4 NON-ISSUE (timer works correctly), #40 VALID minor P3/E1 (job_type not surfaced, ~3 lines), #41 SUPERSEDED (a00a863e), #50 VALID BUG low priority (incorrect timeout in protocol_builder.py, ~15 min fix). Total: 4 items closed (CW-2, CW-4, #41, plus #40 optional), 1 trivial fix needed (#50). Combined with Tier 1 (14 resolved): 20 of 21 Feb report items now resolved. Only VD-1 (vision pipeline) remains as Tier 3.
